title: schoenus subaphyllus
intro: Schoenus subaphyllus, also known as the golden club-sedge, is a type of sedge plant native to Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds and skin irritations due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Schoenus subaphyllus include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, as well as its potential to aid in the healing process of wounds and minor cuts. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
